heroText: Ramallah is a capital city in Palestine, connecting local landmarks, event context, transport access and seasonal reasons to plan a visit.

highlights.name: Ramallah
highlights.text: Ramallah is a capital city in Palestine, connecting local landmarks, event context, transport access and seasonal reasons to plan a visit.

history.label: 16th century
history.text: Ramallah was founded in the 16th century by the Hadadeens, an Arab Christian clan.

history.label: 1500s
history.text: Modern Ramallah was founded in the mid-1500s by the Haddadins (also: Haddadeen), a clan of brothers descended from Ghassanid Christians.
In 1883, the PEF's Survey of Western Palestine described Ramallah as A large Christian village, of well-built stone houses, standing on a high ridge, with a view on the west extending to the sea.
About a quarter of the inhabitants are Roman Catholics, the rest Orthodox Greeks. British Mandate During World War I, the British Army captured and occupied Ramallah in December 1917.
The city became a center of insurgent activity when the 1936–39 Arab revolt in Palestine broke out.
In 1939, the Jerusalem Electric Company introduced electricity to Ramallah, and a majority of the city's homes became wired shortly thereafter.

The Israeli Civil Administration (CA), established in 1981, was in charge of civilian and day-to-day services such as issuing permission to travel, build, export or import, and host relatives from abroad.

A Palestinian passport issued to Ramallah residents was not valid unless the serial number was registered with the Israeli authorities, who controlled border crossings.The failure of the Camp David summit in July 2000 led.

Throughout 2005, while the Disengagement Plan was underway, some US government officials suggested to the Palestinian leadership to move the provisional capital back to Gaza, where it had been when the Palestinian Authority was.

history.label: Today
history.text: Located just south of the built-up area is Tell en-Nasbeh, an archeological site where biblical Mizpah in Benjamin is likely to have been located.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: The rebels subsequently established a court near Ramallah, in order to provide legal alternatives to the courts of the British Mandate.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: According to the Israeli Human Rights activists, the development of Jewish settlements in the Ramallah area, such as Beit El and Psagot, prevented the expansion of the city and cut it off from the.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: A network of bypass roads for use of Israeli citizens only was built around Ramallah, and Israel expropriated land for settlements.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Although considered an interim solution, Ramallah became the de facto capital of the Palestinian Authority, now officially known as the State of Palestine.
